heading: Cost effectiveness calculations
label: 30308 Cost effectiveness calculations

L. 106–391, title III, § 304114 Stat. 1592Oct. 30, 2000In subsection (a), definitions of “commercial provider” and “State” are added to carry forward the appropriate definitions from section 3 of the National Aeronautics and Space Administration Authorization Act of 2000 (, , 1580).Public Law 106–391114 Stat. 1579

children.heading: .—Commercial provider
children.label: 1 .—Commercial provider
children.text: The term “commercial provider” means any person providing space transportation services or other space-related activities, the primary control of which is held by persons other than a Federal, State, local, or foreign government.

children.heading: .—State
children.label: 2 .—State
children.text: The term “State” means each of the several States of the United States, the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, the Virgin Islands, Guam, American Samoa, the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands, and any other commonwealth, territory, or possession of the United States.

children.heading: .—In General
children.label: b .—In General
children.text: Except as otherwise required by law, in calculating the cost effectiveness of the cost of the Administration engaging in an activity as compared to a commercial provider, the Administrator shall compare the cost of the Administration engaging in the activity using full cost accounting principles with the price the commercial provider will charge for such activity.
